content.rendered:
Flat roofing in Calgary has become one of the most popular roofing options among many homeowners in the city. You might wonder: what about it is so appealing? Namely, the biggest reasons are its easy installation and being rather easy on your wallet. However, many might not know that proper ventilation is important to keep flat roofs in good condition. Learn why flat roofing companies in Calgary stress the importance of ventilation for flat roofs here. 

The Importance of Ventilation For Flat Roofing in Calgary

What is Flat Roof Ventilation? 

Flat roof ventilation allows air to circulate within the roofing structure to prevent the buildup of excess moisture. If moisture gets inside due to poor ventilation, all flat roofing in Calgary is vulnerable to forming mold and mildew. Not only does this lead to paying costly repairs to flat roofing companies in Calgary to fix damages, but it can also create poor insulation that will make you see your energy bill skyrocket. 

Flat Roof Ventilation Options 

There are several different types of flat roof ventilation options available. The most common type is ridge venting, which involves installing vents along the roof ridge to allow air to flow in and out of the roof. Other options include soffit vents installed along the roof’s eaves and turbine vents powered by the wind. Depending on your roof, installing a combination of these different vents may be necessary to ensure proper ventilation.

Signs Flat Roofing in Calgary Needs Ventilation

One of the tell-tale signs of a flat roof in need of ventilation is the presence of mold and mildew. If you notice any discoloration on the underside of the roof, it may be a sign that moisture is getting trapped and needs to be vented out. Additionally, if you’ve noticed that the temperature inside your home is higher than usual, it could be a sign that your roof isn’t getting enough ventilation.

Flat Roof Ventilation Requirements in Calgary 

If you have a flat roof, you must know that there are flat roof ventilation requirements you must adhere to, according to the city. In Calgary, flat roofs must meet certain ventilation requirements to be safe and compliant. The Canadian Building Code requires that all flat roofs have a minimum of 1 square foot of ventilation per 150 square feet of roof space. The ventilation must be evenly distributed throughout the roof to ensure proper air circulation.

Maintenance & Repair for Flat Roof Ventilation 

Once the flat roof ventilation has been installed, it’s important to keep an eye on it to ensure it’s functioning properly. After all, you don’t want a poor installation to lead to damage. That’s why being on top of maintenance is important. Another good idea is to have the vents regularly inspected to ensure they aren’t blocked by debris or damaged by extreme weather. And knowing Calgary’s unpredictable weather patterns, this has a high chance of occurring. If any issues are found, they should be addressed right away to avoid further damage.

City Roofing & Exteriors consistently takes pride in serving Calgarians. At City Roofing, we understand the importance of customer satisfaction. We put safety, quality work and on time delivery as our top priority. We work hard to assist our customers in providing proper roofing systems to meet their budget and needs. We offer completed roofing services in Calgary and area on both residential and commercial projects. We guarantee that you will be satisfied with our work and recommend City Roofing as your roofing consultant and contractor. Give us a call at 403-608-9933 or send an email to cityroofing111@gmail.com.
